items.title: The controversial $1m Fitzroy River plan is closing for consultation. What is it?
items.summary: Public submissions for the million-dollar draft Fitzroy-Derby water management plan close today. Here's everything you need to know.

items.title: Retiring Ben Stokes backs Harry Brook as England's next Test captain
items.summary: Ben Stokes says vice-captain Harry Brook has his "100 per cent" support to lead England's Test side, but coach Brendon McCullum is less effusive.

items.title: World Cup: How a punt on a trial in Italy turned Cristian Volpato into the next Socceroos star
items.summary: Cristian Volpato was rejected by two Australian soccer academies. His family dropped everything to go for a trial in Italy. That was the making of Australia's next star.

items.title: Jannik Sinner survives Wimbledon scare, Aryna Sabalenka coasts, Naomi Osaka rocks kimono
items.summary: Jannik Sinner is pushed to the brink and Aryna Sabalenka coasts into the second round as both top seeds bounce back from disappointment at the French Open.

items.title: Police probe ramraid and suspicious fire in Melbourne's south-east
items.summary: Investigators believe unknown offenders rammed a vehicle into a shop in Mentone overnight before a fire gutted the business.

items.title: Tourism operator wants more information on recognising bird flu amid sick bird sightings
items.summary: A tourism operator on South Australia's coast is frustrated by what he says is poor communication from authorities about bird flu. He says he saw sick birds weeks ago but did not know to report the sighting.

items.title: Maree Vermont's sons speak of devastation after Airbnb guest found guilty of killing her
items.summary: Maree Vermont's sons say they never thought hosting Airbnb guests would be dangerous for their mother, who was proud of the home she had built.

items.title: Daria Kasatkina wins Wimbledon opener against local wildcard Mimi Xu
items.summary: After a winless grass-court swing, Australian Daria Kasatkina becomes Australia's first winner at Wimbledon for 2026.

items.title: Coroner rules hiker's death in Mount Canobolas bushland not suspicious, praises 12-day search
items.summary: The NSW coroner praises emergency services for "persistence" during the 12-day search for a hiker who set off on an ill-fated midnight mountain walk in sub-zero temperatures while wearing sandals.

items.title: Seven Australians in Venezuela request help as earthquake death toll rises
items.summary: Australia is providing "consular assistance" to seven people after Venezuela's two earthquakes, Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong confirms.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic was increasing until a fresh wave of strikes
items.summary: Fresh attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz have exposed the fragility of the 60-day ceasefire deal between the US and Iran, which was meant to allow ships to transit through the waterway again.

items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia from UK
items.summary: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after she was slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

items.title: Simpson Desert in bloom but Birdsville's tourism season in limbo
items.summary: Birdsville, a pilgrimage point for outback travellers, navigates the ripple effects of flood, fuel fears and the cancellation of a massive music festival, when the surrounding country has rarely looked so green and alive.

items.title: One person is charged a fortnight as Queensland police crack down on lethal homemade guns
items.summary: Since Queensland strengthened its firearm laws in March, one person a fortnight has been charged with possessing a blueprint to make 3D-printed guns.

items.title: Worker says Victoria's forestry transition program 'decimated' Gippsland's small towns
items.summary: The Victorian Forestry Transition Program is ending. In the regions that relied on the timber trade, the question is whether it worked.

items.title: ABC Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell announces his retirement after more than a quarter of a century
items.summary: After decades of keeping night owls and shift workers company on ABC radio across Australia, Overnights presenter Trevor Chappell decides to call it a day at the age of 65.

items.title: Are the wheels falling off the AI investment boom?
items.summary: Overshadowing the tumult that is global politics has been a financial boom beyond almost anything witnessed before, focused entirely on the perceived benefits wrought by a brave new world of AI that threatens to up-end our lives.

items.title: US Supreme Court rejects Donald Trump's final appeal against E Jean Carroll verdict
items.summary: The US Supreme Court declined to hear US President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5 million USD verdict in a case in which a jury found him liable for sexually abusing and defaming E Jean Carroll.

items.title: Private health group Ramsay given special access to public hospital emergency patients
items.summary: A trial to ease pressure on a public emergency department has raised concerns a multi-million-dollar company is getting a special deal.

items.title: CTE poses a massive dilemma for the AFL. Experts say urgent action is needed to protect players

items.title: 'Systemic racism' claims identified in buried reef authority review
items.summary: The damning results of a review into the agency tasked with managing the Great Barrier Reef, an area bigger than New Zealand and home to 70 traditional owner groups, has never before seen the light of day.

items.title: Lodging too early and other mistakes people make when doing their taxes
items.summary: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

items.title: Ariane Beeston critiques ballet's toxic culture in new YA novel, Don't Forget to Smile

items.title: Flag quiz, part 4: Will you raise the white flag on these tricky questions?

items.title: Sam Kerr's future confirmed as she signs for Gotham FC

items.title: To the Manor Born star Dame Penelope Keith dead at 86

items.title: Aidan Becker plaque unveiled to remember man who died at Mernda train station while trying to save teenager
items.summary: The plaque remembers 22-year-old Aidan Becker, who was killed when trying to rescue a 14-year-old boy from an alleged machete attack at Mernda train station in March.

items.summary: Billy Slater says he will not sign another contract to be Queensland State of Origin coach, but that does not mean he will not continue in the role.

items.summary: An alleged victim of cult leader William "Little Pebble" Kamm has told a court she began experiencing extreme anxiety as she pondered an escape from a property on the NSW South Coast.

items.title: Brother tells of family's 'unimaginable pain' after doctor's death in Tasmania
items.summary: The brother of Royal Hobart Hospital doctor Artyom Avetisyan says the cardiothoracic surgery registrar sounded "extremely exhausted" in the week before his crash death.

items.title: Missing Darkan woman found safe after five-day community-led search
items.summary: The desperate search for a 46-year-old woman who disappeared in southern Western Australia last week ends with her being found safe and well.

items.title: Superannuation switching trend sees billions flow out of retail funds, at a risk
items.summary: There is now more than $1 trillion in self-managed superannuation funds, which means a big chunk of money isn't regulated by APRA, and it's the rate of the shift into SMSFs and less-regulated managed investment schemes that has regulators worried.

items.title: Father charged with murder and torture of four-month-old baby to remain in custody
items.summary: Police say the baby's condition was consistent with "a child-shaking injury".

items.title: Winton's film festival kicks off as Red Dog director reveals new thriller
items.summary: Red Dog director Kriv Stenders reveals plans for a new thriller film as the annual Vision Splendid Outback Film Festival kicks off in Winton.

items.title: Serena Williams criticises tennis anti-doping protocol ahead of Wimbledon return
items.summary: As she prepares for her All England Club comeback, Serena Williams claims the tennis anti-doping protocol is "unprofessional" and "unreasonable".

items.title: Canberra man found guilty of attempted murder after stabbing his wife in violent home attack
items.summary: An ACT Supreme Court jury has found a Canberra man guilty of attempted murder after he stabbed his wife during a violent attack in the family's Gungahlin home.

items.title: WA regional courts to reopen a month earlier than planned after security issues
items.summary: Regional West Australian courts closed amid a stoush over security services are set to reopen about a month earlier than originally planned.

items.summary: US space agency NASA is racing to save an aging telescope, used to observe cosmic explosions, from falling back to Earth with a daring rescue mission.

items.title: Ben Stokes retirement highlights his main character energy was not always a force for good
items.summary: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

items.title: World Cup: Has the chaotic, but engaging group stage justified the expanded tournament?
items.summary: Ghana's coach Carlos Queiroz has lashed out at FIFA's expanded 48-team World Cup, despite his team progressing. Is his criticism justified as the tournament moves into the knockout stage?

items.title: France records 1,000 excess daily deaths as Europe's record heatwave sparks power outages and wildfires
items.summary: More than 150 million Europeans swelter in "extreme heat", which scientists say has been the worst the continent has ever recorded.
